operating_state: Consolidated rental revenues increased to $6.1 billion for nine months ended September 30, 2025, driven by positive rent change of 52.0% on lease rollover and sustained occupancy at 95.2% across the O&M portfolio. Real Estate Segment NOI grew year-over-year from rent increases on lease renewals and development completions. Strategic Capital revenues reached $439 million with operating distributions from unconsolidated entities totaling $455 million versus $417 million in 2024.
cost_structure: Rental expenses increased to $1.5 billion from $1.3 billion reflecting portfolio expansion and higher property management costs, while general and administrative expenses rose. Interest payments net of capitalized amounts rose significantly to $711 million from $573 million reflecting higher debt service costs. Equity-based compensation expenses decreased while depreciation and amortization expenses remained stable at $2.0 billion.
material_events: The company issued $2.9 billion in senior notes across multiple currencies with weighted average interest rate of 4.3% and 9-year maturity. Amended and restated a global credit facility extending maturity to June 2029 and established an additional commercial paper program. Generated $362 million in net proceeds from property dispositions primarily from contributions to European co-investment ventures, while Lori Palazzolo announced retirement as Chief Accounting Officer effective April 2026.
macro_exposures: Economic uncertainty from tariff policy proposals introduced in April created disruption, though leasing activity remained resilient with 31 million square feet of new consolidated leases signed in Q3. Foreign currency exposure across 20 countries creates translation risk partially mitigated through functional currency borrowing and derivative instruments. Trade tensions and shifting policies present ongoing uncertainty for customer decision-making and supply chain positioning.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued revenue growth driven by 19% lease mark-to-market opportunity across the O&M portfolio enabling rent increases. Development pipeline includes potential from consolidated land holdings with majority of new starts being build-to-suit projects requiring additional investment. Strategic focus on data center conversions and expanding Prologis Essentials platform, while quarterly common stock dividend increased to $1.01 from $0.96 per share year-over-year.
uncertainty_profile: Potential impact of ongoing economic uncertainty and trade policy changes on customer behavior remains difficult to predict, while ongoing negotiations for property acquisitions and dispositions create execution timing uncertainty. Development portfolio completion requires additional investment with pre-leasing across properties under construction. Unconsolidated co-investment ventures carry third-party debt with 6-year weighted average maturity, though company provides no guarantees.
